topic_definition: Language indicating that a senior engineer is expected to teach, grow, coach, and shepherd other engineers through influence rather than formal authority. Captures pair programming, code-reading and review-as-teaching, onboarding, knowledge transfer, architecture guidance, and explicit learning culture. Distinct from People-management (formal reports, perf reviews, hiring/firing) and Verification (review/testing as correctness gate).

topic_exclusions.pattern: user guide / style guide / installation guide / api guide / quick start guide
topic_exclusions.reason: 'guide' as a noun referring to documentation, not a person guiding engineers.
topic_exclusions.guard: Match 'guide(s|d|ing)' only as a verb directed at people, or require proximity (≤4 tokens) to engineers/team/peers/junior/others; exclude when preceded by 'user', 'style', 'install*', 'API', 'quick start', 'getting started' (unless 'getting started' is paired with onboarding context).

topic_exclusions.pattern: coaching benefits / executive coaching / health coaching / wellness coach
topic_exclusions.reason: 'coach/coaching' often appears in benefits sections referring to perks, not engineer-on-engineer coaching.
topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude when within 5 tokens of 'benefit', 'perk', 'wellness', 'health', 'fitness', 'executive', 'life coach', 'career coach' (the last is ambiguous — keep if context is clearly internal mentorship).

topic_exclusions.pattern: lead engineer / lead developer / leads the team / leads project
topic_exclusions.reason: 'Lead' is overloaded: title noun, project lead, sales lead, lead generation. Only 'lead by example' / 'lead through influence' / 'tech lead' clearly indicate mentorship.
topic_exclusions.guard: Match only specific phrases: 'lead by example', 'leading by example', 'lead through influence', 'leadership through influence', 'tech lead', 'technical lead'. Do not count bare 'lead'/'leads'/'leading'.

topic_exclusions.pattern: performance review / business review / security review / compliance review
topic_exclusions.reason: 'Review' alone covers many non-mentorship activities including formal perf reviews (which belong to People-management).
topic_exclusions.guard: Anchor 'review' to 'code', 'design', 'architecture', 'architectural', 'arch', 'PR', 'pull request', 'RFC', 'spec', 'peer'. Exclude 'performance review', 'perf review', 'business review', 'compliance review', 'security review' (latter belongs to Verification).

topic_exclusions.pattern: customer onboarding / user onboarding / client onboarding
topic_exclusions.reason: 'Onboarding' frequently refers to customer/user flows, especially in product/growth roles.
topic_exclusions.guard: Exclude when within 5 tokens of 'customer', 'user', 'client', 'merchant', 'partner', 'vendor', 'patient'. Keep only when paired with 'engineer', 'developer', 'hire', 'team', 'employee', 'new joiner'.

topic_exclusions.reason: 'Ramp up' has a generic business meaning (scale up production/sales).
topic_exclusions.guard: Require proximity to 'engineer', 'developer', 'new hire', 'team', 'newcomer', or to 'onboarding' within ±10 tokens.

topic_calibration_recommendations: 1) Sample 200 postings stratified by year (2024/2025/2026) and seniority (junior/mid/senior/staff+); hand-label as mentorship-positive/negative. Compute precision/recall per concept and per keyword to identify weak terms. 2) Pay special attention to 'guide', 'coach', 'lead', 'review', 'onboarding', 'pairing', 'champion' — each has a high false-positive base rate. Implement the proximity guards in `exclusions` before counting. 3) Compute baseline density at junior level: mentorship markers should be near-zero in IC-junior postings and rise sharply at senior/staff/principal. If baseline is non-trivial, guards are too loose. 4) Consider a two-pass approach: Pass 1 unrestricted match -> Pass 2 apply exclusion guards. Report both for sensitivity analysis. 5) Track Tier-1 'Direct mentorship language' (mentor*) as the gold-signal anchor; other concepts should correlate with it within senior cells.
